title: Add tests + banner + /lessons:handoff command for anti-compact feature
description: Tests for precompact-handoff.mjs (unit + integration), context-full banner on disabled path, LESSONS_HANDOFF_ONLY mode for on-demand handoffs, /lessons:handoff command with auto/on/off subcommands.
close_reason: Extracted pure functions to hooks/lib/precompact.mjs, added LESSONS_HANDOFF_ONLY mode, context-full banner on disabled path, 24 unit tests + 13 integration tests, /lessons:handoff command

title: Pre-compact hook: emit token count + fenced handoff prompt before /compact fires
description:
Wire a PreCompact hook (investigate if Claude Code supports this event — check hooks.json event types and Claude Code docs) that intercepts /compact before it runs. Hook emits: current token count + a fenced markdown copy-paste handoff prompt summarizing session state (active issues, recent changes, next steps) so the user can paste into a new session and continue without context loss. All emitted prompts must be fenced markdown blocks. Trial in this project first; extract to standalone plugin if successful.

FALLBACK CONDITION: If PreCompact fires too late (context already severely degraded by the time the hook runs), fall back to earlier-trigger research. Options: (1) systemd/launchd service monitoring session JSONL for token growth and alerting before degradation; (2) in-process token counting heuristic in the hook layer with a configurable threshold; (3) scheduled compact trigger. Only pursue fallback path if pre-compact hook timing proves insufficient.
close_reason: Created hooks/precompact-handoff.mjs + added PreCompact entry to hooks.json; blocks compaction with quality handoff via claude -p + fallback

title: Fix claude-mem MCP: memory not being captured or injected
description: claude-mem (thedotmack plugin v10.5.2) shows prompts in dashboard but creates no memories. Root causes identified: (1) mcp-search MCP server defined in plugin .mcp.json but NOT registered in ~/.claude/settings.json mcpServers — Claude Code tools unavailable; (2) Chroma vector DB at ~/Library/Application Support/mcp-memory/ last modified Aug 2025, suggesting PostToolUse observation hook is silently failing; (3) no error logging on hook failures. Fix: register MCP server explicitly in settings.json, add hook error logging, verify DB write access.
close_reason: Registered mcp-search MCP server in settings.json and merged all claude-mem hooks (SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PostToolUse, Stop) with absolute fallback paths

title: Discover PXE server layout and fill in pi-* script placeholders
description: SSH into pxe server, capture df/ls/exports/services output. Fill in NFS_ROOT, TFTP_ROOT, PXE_SERVER in ~/bin/pi-bind-mounts; NODES in ~/bin/pi-status; NODE_IP/NODE_ROLE in ~/bin/pi-cloud-init. Update pi-cluster.md Cluster State table with real paths.
close_reason: PXE layout fully discovered: TFTP=/tftpboot, NFS=/tftpboot/images, pi5-1/pi5-2 confirmed PXE booting, all scripts filled in

title: Preserve EEPROM recovery image: copy from /tmp to /var/lib/pxe/images + local Mac
description: The recovery image at /tmp/eeprom-recovery-sd/ on the PXE server is in a 449MB tmpfs and will be lost on reboot. Copy to /var/lib/pxe/images (persistent) and scp to ~/Downloads/pi-eeprom/ locally for flashing.
notes: Recovery image was lost from /tmp before session. pi5-1 and pi5-2 already have correct EEPROM (0xf21). Image only needed if pi5-3 requires EEPROM recovery. Use pi-eeprom-recovery to re-download if needed.
close_reason: Not recoverable (tmpfs cleared), but pi5-1/pi5-2 EEPROM already correct. pi5-3 status TBD.

title: MCP server: complete brainstorm → implementation plan
description: Brainstorming session for the MCP server feature (PRD at docs/prds/003-mcp-server.md) was interrupted mid-session. Need to: (1) finish the brainstorming Q&A (proxy diagram was the last open question, user understood it), (2) write a proper implementation plan (not a PRD copy) in the plan file, (3) call ExitPlanMode so user can comment inline and approve. Key decisions already made: transparent proxy architecture, sibling mcp/ package with own package.json, shared data/ directory (SQLite + manifest), Desktop Commander tool name mapping via normalize-tool.mjs.

title: Group lesson candidates by tag in review/approval output
description: When lessons review surfaces candidates, group them by shared tags instead of DB insertion order. Reviewers working thematically can spot overlapping or redundant lessons more easily, deprioritize or skip duplicates, and move faster through related items. Tag grouping mirrors the planned injection grouping (tag-cluster injection issue) for consistency.

title: Add authoritative preamble before injected lesson list at session start
description: Prepend a short framing paragraph before the lesson list in session-start injection that establishes them as high-priority, hard-won rules — similar to how superpowers skills use authoritative, hard-gate framing. Goal: the model treats them as non-negotiable without every individual lesson needing to repeat that framing. Reference the superpowers:using-superpowers skill for framing examples.
close_reason: Split directive vs protocol preambles in session-start-lesson-protocol.mjs — directives get IMPORTANT wrapper, protocols get coordination framing

title: Group session-start lessons by tag clusters in injection output
description: Instead of emitting lessons in DB order at session start, cluster them by shared tags (tool:X, severity:Y, topic:Z) so the model receives thematically coherent blocks. Explore multi-tag overlap handling (e.g., a lesson matching two clusters — assign to primary tag or emit in both).

title: Create lesson nursery: holding area for lessons with no viable injection path yet
description:
Some lessons encode valid knowledge but can't be injected yet because the current matching system doesn't support their trigger pattern (no content-based matching, no project-type scoping). Rather than leaving them as noisy global protocols or deleting them, create a designated place for collecting these cases.

What the nursery is:
- A status value or tag that marks a lesson as excluded from injection
- Lessons are preserved for pattern discovery and future use
- Accumulating examples will surface patterns for new injection mechanisms

Current nursery candidates:
- process-management (ex: child.unref): needs content-aware matching (detect spawn/Popen patterns in source being edited)
- local-plugin: needs project-type scoping (detect: is this repo a Claude Code plugin?)

Open questions:
- Status value ('nursery') vs tag ('in:nursery') vs separate table?
- How does bd list / bd doctor report nursery contents?
- Should nursery lessons still appear in bd list or be hidden?
- What's the graduation criteria to move out of nursery?

Related: lessons-learned-5ny (injection archetypes investigation)
close_reason: GitHub issue opened at https://github.com/joeblackwaslike/lessons-learned/issues/1; two lessons archived to nursery with in:nursery tag

title: Lesson citation format: include summary+problem+solution when referencing multiple lessons
description: When Claude references multiple lessons by ID in a single response, the output often just lists IDs (e.g. 'see lesson #42, #43') with no inline content. Readers can't act on bare IDs. Citations should inline the summary+problem+solution so the context is visible without a separate lookup.

title: Add serial console guidance to pi-cluster.md skill
description: pi-cluster.md has no mention of serial console. Serial is the fastest debug tool — shows BOOT_ORDER and exact failure point immediately, while SSH/ping/dnsmasq checks give no signal when a node fails to boot. Document: device /dev/cu.usbmodem112402, baud 115200, non-interactive read pattern, and rule: always check serial first when a node fails to boot or reach the network.

title: lessons-learned plugin features: /help command, /onboard command, doctor automation
description: Session 41624768 was actively building these features but ended with 'continue' prompts without confirmed completion. Features: (1) /help slash command for config/command discovery; (2) /onboard command to load initial lessons from JSON with per-lesson approval workflow; (3) doctor command automated scheduling (run weekly, auto-trigger after /lessons:review). Verify which are implemented vs only designed before starting.
close_reason: Implemented: /help already existed. /onboard: new CLI subcommand + command file. Doctor auto-trigger already in review.md Phase 8. Weekly scheduling filed as lessons-learned-y0d.

title: Tag/schema taxonomy audit: rename types, fix 13 hints→directives
description: Session 27f83720 did a comprehensive audit of lesson types and schema consistency but was interrupted. Key findings: 13 'hint' lessons should be reclassified as 'directive' type; field renames under discussion (mistake→problem, remediation→solution). The audit examined lessons.schema.json vs manifest.schema.json alignment. Work was paused mid-discussion of why the 13 hints-to-directives reclassification matters.
close_reason: Reclassified 8 behavioral hints as directives. Field renames were already done. 11 directives total, 47 hints, 193 tests passing.

title: Set up PXE infrastructure: NFS root export + TFTP boot files per node
description: Configure NFS root filesystem per node under NFS_ROOT/<node>/. Add to /etc/exports. Populate TFTP dirs by Pi serial (short serial = last 8 hex chars). Verify bind mounts with pi-bind-mounts.

title: Set up PXE infrastructure: NFS root export + TFTP boot files per node
description: Configure NFS root filesystem per node under NFS_ROOT/<node>/. Add to /etc/exports. Populate TFTP dirs by Pi serial (short serial = last 8 hex chars). Verify bind mounts with pi-bind-mounts.
close_reason: All three complete: EEPROM 0xf21 confirmed on pi5-3, PXE booting as pi-3, cloud-init status done

title: Flash EEPROM recovery image on pi1, pi2, pi3
description: Two-flash procedure: flash 2712-sd.img to SD card, boot each Pi (green LED pattern then auto-off), remove SD. Verify EEPROM bootloader_config shows network in BOOT_ORDER. Requires physical access to each node.
notes: Session 5eb75583 was interrupted on Apr 17 02:43 UTC. pi5-1 and pi5-2 are DONE (BOOT_ORDER=0xf21, online). pi5-3 (serial: 6b91f29e) is the only remaining node. Recovery SD card may still be inserted — physical state unknown. Recovery image: rpi-boot-eeprom-recovery-*-2712-sd.img (NOT 2711). Target: BOOT_ORDER=0xf21. Verify with: vcgencmd bootloader_config | grep BOOT_ORDER via Pi Debug Probe serial console.
close_reason: All three complete: EEPROM 0xf21 confirmed on pi5-3, PXE booting as pi-3, cloud-init status done

title: Audit and document cross-tool compatibility (Codex, Gemini, Kimi, OpenClaw, etc.)
description: Map which plugin features require Claude Code specifically vs can run in any agent environment: hooks (SessionStart, PreToolUse, SubagentStart), session-start injection output, #lesson tag scanning, CLI (lessons.mjs), lesson-manifest.json format. Identify gaps and what would be needed to support each platform. Document a compatibility matrix. Cross-reference lessons-learned-a3v (community engagement) — cross-tool compatibility is a prerequisite for broader community adoption.

title: Spike: SQLite vector embeddings for lesson detection in transcript scanner
description:
Explore adding a vector column (sqlite-vec or sqlite-vss) to lessons.db to embed session transcript messages and detect lesson-worthy moments semantically. Use as supplement or replacement for heuristic Tier 2 scanning.

Key dimension: vector/embedding approach is likely significantly more token-conservative than current heuristics — embeddings + similarity search do heavy lifting without feeding large transcript chunks to the model. This is a major advantage for keeping scanning fast and cheap.

Evaluate: accuracy vs current heuristics, false positive rate, embedding model options (local vs API, cost vs quality tradeoff), token cost reduction vs current Tier 2, integration with CLI tooling to minimize context consumption. Could also surface pre-tag-era lessons from old logs that heuristics missed.

title: Evaluate merging protocol and directive lesson types; add per-type intro paragraphs
description: Audit whether protocol and directive lesson types are meaningfully distinct or effectively redundant. Decide: merge into one type with clear semantics, or keep both with explicit distinguishing criteria documented. Either way, add a short introductory paragraph before each type group in session-start output to orient the model on how to interpret each group.

title: Add 5 additional anti-pattern checks to doctor/preflight (trigger-reality drift, staleness, overspecified trigger, context bleed, priority homogeneity)
description:
Five additional lesson quality anti-patterns identified that have no current automated check. Each needs a detection strategy and implementation in bd doctor / bd lint.

Anti-patterns:
1. Trigger-reality drift: commandPattern/pathPattern was accurate when written but no longer matches current usage (command renamed, path restructured). Lesson technically compiles but never fires.
2. Solution staleness: solution references an API, flag, or option that no longer exists. Injects actively wrong guidance.
3. Overspecified trigger: pattern matches only one invocation form, missing equivalents (e.g., 'git stash' but not 'git stash push'). Fires rarely in practice.
4. Context bleed: problem/solution uses session-specific shorthand only parseable if you were in the original session. Confusing to the model and to reviewers.
5. Priority homogeneity: many lessons at same priority+type — injection ordering within the cluster is arbitrary.

Some of these (staleness, drift) require execution data (fire rate tracking) rather than static analysis. File sub-issues as the approach for each becomes clear.

See docs/architecture/quality-checks.md for full anti-pattern catalog. Depends on: lessons-learned-4ef (baseline checks), lessons-learned-nwe (intake validation).

title: Document manual lesson entry by type with concrete examples
description:
People entering lessons manually (via lessons add or lessons edit --patch) don't know which fields are required per type, or that some field combinations are contradictory (e.g., directive+toolNames).

Create a doc (e.g., docs/contributing-lessons.md or expand CLAUDE.md) with:
- One concrete example per lesson type (hint, guard, directive, protocol)
- The directive vs hint distinction explained with a before/after showing the toolNames pitfall
- Which fields are required vs optional per type
- What commandMatchTarget: 'executable' does and when to use it
- Common mistakes and how to avoid them

Extend this to also cover best practices for:
- Capturing lessons: what makes a good lesson, when to emit #lesson tags, how to frame problem/solution for maximum compliance
- Reviewing and promoting candidates: how to evaluate quality, spot duplicates, decide priority
- Optimizing existing lessons: how to tune summaries, triggers, and patterns for better compliance and fewer false positives

This is a companion to the intake validation work (lessons-learned-nwe) — documentation prevents problems that can't yet be caught by automated checks.

title: Investigate intake and lifecycle validation to catch anti-patterns at earliest possible stage
description:
Anti-patterns that exist in the lesson store are harder to fix than ones caught on entry. This issue tracks investigating where to add validation at each lifecycle stage.

Anti-patterns to prevent (full reference in docs/architecture/quality-checks.md):

1. directive+toolNames misconfiguration: lessons add / lessons edit should warn or block if type=directive and toolNames is non-empty (toolNames is silently ignored for directives — should be hint instead)
2. Dead trigger: hint/guard with commandPatterns or pathPatterns but no toolNames — will never fire
3. Weak problem-solution pair: solution word-count floor + high word-overlap ratio with problem (solution just restates problem)
4. Global scope for narrow concept: heuristic — flag when tags or summary suggest domain-specific knowledge but scope is null
5. Project-type/content-aware detection mechanisms: future — detect repo type at inject time to scope lessons dynamically

Lifecycle stages to add checks:
- lessons add (intake) — block or warn
- scan promote — same checks before promoting candidates
- lessons edit (patch) — re-validate affected fields
- bd lint / bd doctor — batch audit of existing store
- bd preflight — pre-PR gate

Goal: prevent anti-patterns from entering the store rather than discovering them in post-hoc audits.

title: Identify ways to engage the community in building this plugin
description:
This plugin has value beyond a single user's session history. Community involvement could:

- Contribute lessons from their own sessions (curated lesson packs)
- Propose and implement injection strategies for nursery archetypes
- Add support for new lesson types or matching mechanisms
- Share project-type-specific lesson sets (e.g., Django dev, Kubernetes, etc.)

What to figure out:
- Best format for submitting community lessons (PR? issue template? lesson pack file?)
- How to curate/review community submissions without quality degrading
- What contribution docs / contributing.md need to say
- Should there be a community lessons registry separate from personal lessons.db?

A GitHub issue should be opened alongside this beads issue to make the invitation public and gather external ideas — especially around the nursery problem (lessons that exist but have no viable trigger).

title: Study two unresolved lesson injection archetypes: topic/content-aware and project-keyword-matching
description: Two injection patterns were identified but not implemented: (1) topic/content-aware -- inject lessons based on what Claude is writing about, not just which tool is called; (2) project-keyword-matching -- match lessons against project-specific terms (e.g. beads, dolt) regardless of tool. Need to study feasibility, hook API surface, and whether these are best modeled as lesson types or matcher extensions.

title: Add lesson quality audit checks to doctor/preflight command
description:
doctor and preflight currently check sync state and orphans. They don't validate lesson quality: missing toolNames (lesson can never fire), summary over 80 chars (truncation), placeholder text left in fields, or zero-match lessons. Adding these checks would surface dead lessons before they waste injection budget.

Also extend to include a protocol message audit: examine the #lesson tag emission instructions injected at session start (session-start-lesson-protocol.mjs). Audit for compliance rate and coverage — are lessons being emitted for a wide range of problem/solution sequences, or are they only triggered in narrow cases? Iteratively improve the instruction framing to increase compliance and detect a wider range of lessons. Measure improvement by scanning session logs before/after.

title: Doctor weekly scheduling via CronCreate
description: Weekly automated doctor audit is missing. The lessons:doctor command and its auto-trigger after lessons:review are implemented, but there is no weekly schedule. Implement using CronCreate to schedule a recurring Claude Code session that runs /lessons:doctor and optionally reports results.

title: E2E tests and config: resume incomplete test/config work from session f50e54c8
description: Session f50e54c8 (moonlit-bubbling-deer) examined config.json, E2E tests, and CLI tools across multiple 'continue' cycles without confirmed completion. Needs: read last 200 lines of session to identify exact stopping point, verify current test pass rate, identify specific config or E2E gaps left open.

title: New lesson type: knowledge/model override for correcting Claude's built-in assumptions
description: Session a8f370e2 designed a new lesson type that lets users override or tune Claude's internal knowledge and default skill behaviors. Example use case: correcting superpowers:creating-plans behavior in a way that persists across skill upgrades. Design was in discovery phase when interrupted. Needs: finalized type spec, schema changes, injection mechanics, and a worked example.

title: Configure cloud-init for each cluster node
description: Run pi-cloud-init for pi-1, pi-2, pi-3. Copy output to each node boot partition. Verify cloud-init status on first PXE boot.
close_reason: All three complete: EEPROM 0xf21 confirmed on pi5-3, PXE booting as pi-3, cloud-init status done

title: Install k3s: server on pi1, agents on pi2+pi3
description: Install k3s server on pi-1. Capture node token. Install k3s agent on pi-2 and pi-3 pointing at pi-1:6443. Verify with kubectl get nodes showing all 3 Ready.

title: Brainstorm lesson injection system from first principles
description: The current injection system uses 4 types: hint (trigger-based), guard (block-on-trigger), protocol (session-start), directive. Two issues surfaced during the lesson audit: (1) 11 hint lessons had no triggers and were effectively dead, requiring reclassification; (2) trigger-based injection only works for tool/command patterns — there is no support for task-contextual injection (e.g., 'relevant when planning a batch') or multi-level injection (session-start + pre-tool). This task is to brainstorm from first principles: what are all the moments where lesson injection can affect agent behavior, and what injection model best covers them? Topics: task-contextual triggers, SubagentStart injection, injection at plan entry, combining triggers with session-start for the same lesson, and whether summary should be computed from problem rather than stored separately.

title: Redundancy sweep: make type the authoritative signal everywhere
description:
After the type taxonomy PR stabilises, audit all interfaces to ensure type is the single source of truth across the injection pipeline. A developer should only need to understand type to understand full injection behavior.

Scope:
- manifest keys/shape — ensure no legacy fields leak through
- hook branching — all conditional logic should key off type, not derived booleans
- CLI output (list, add confirmation) — show type prominently
- docs (architecture/data-model.md) — reflect new taxonomy
- any remaining references to injectOn, block, blockReason, or injection in comments/docs
close_reason: All changes implemented: sessionStart removed from manifest+hook, legacy patchable fields removed from db.mjs, manifest schema fixed (type enum + message field), docs updated with type taxonomy, tests all pass

title: Architectural eval: unified ecosystem map of evaluation frameworks
description: Two parallel sessions (86d95763, cc89996a) both attempted to create a unified ecosystem map across 4 architectural evaluation documents — listing each tool/framework with what it does, strengths, limitations, surfacing model, and relevance to PRDs. Both interrupted before completion. Context recovery needed before resuming.
